frontiers.label: Output/topologia
frontiers.mitigation: Keep product apps under products/<slug>/ and maintain .codex-loop/factory/project-registry.json.

frontiers.label: Runtime/toolchain
frontiers.evidence: codex=true; execHelp=0; node=true; npm=true; rg=false; rgShim=true; timeout=900000; retries=3
frontiers.mitigation: Expose Codex/Node/npm on PATH, verify codex exec --help, keep local tool shims ready, and use long timeouts plus at least 3 retries.

frontiers.label: Estado/locks
frontiers.mitigation: Use run.lock, stale detection and unfinished-run detection before starting another factory.

frontiers.label: Calidad real
frontiers.mitigation: Add build/test/coverage scripts and repair until coverage evidence approaches 80%.

frontiers.label: Render/browser
frontiers.mitigation: Frontend products need a real navigation/render smoke path, not only build.

frontiers.label: Packaging
frontiers.mitigation: Only claim a desktop/binary artifact when the packaging config/script or actual artifact exists.

frontiers.label: Secretos/datos
frontiers.mitigation: Use placeholders/env.example only; block generated credential-shaped values in changed files.

frontiers.label: Licencias/GitHub Scout
frontiers.mitigation: Use GitHub for patterns first; adapt only permissive compatible code and record attribution.

frontiers.label: Research trust
frontiers.mitigation: Treat market/web findings as noisy; store source signals, confidence and caveats.

frontiers.label: Coste/tiempo
frontiers.mitigation: Forever mode needs a supervisor guard; finite mode needs an explicit budget or task cap.

frontiers.label: Naming
frontiers.mitigation: Use stable kebab-case product slugs and avoid third-party brands in product identity.

frontiers.label: Rollback
frontiers.mitigation: Keep snapshots or Git available before autonomous workers edit product files.

frontiers.label: Final truth gate
frontiers.mitigation: Do not declare done until build/test/render/packaging/data/license evidence is present or residual risk is explicit.

missingEvidence: Policy-safe wording scan found risky phrasing in planning files.

He dejado el contrato CI/CD alineado entre Codex, Claude, GitHub Copilot y LangChain sin tocar los prompts canónicos ni subir el coste fuera del presupuesto. Añadí un `runtimeEquivalenceContract` en [spec.json](<C:/Users/abaratas/Desktop/CARPETA PROYECTOS/TEST CODEX/NuevoProyecto/dist/copilots/cicd/shared/spec.json:264>), la misma sección de paridad en [Codex](<C:/Users/abaratas/Desktop/CARPETA PROYECTOS/TEST CODEX/NuevoProyecto/dist/copilots/cicd/codex/AGENT.md:14>), [Claude](<C:/Users/abaratas/Desktop/CARPETA PROYECTOS/TEST CODEX/NuevoProyecto/dist/copilots/cicd/claude/AGENT.md:14>) y [GitHub Copilot](<C:/Users/abaratas/Desktop/CARPETA PROYECTOS/TEST CODEX/NuevoProyecto/dist/copilots/cicd/github-copilot/copilot-agent.md:14>), y reforcé LangChain con contrato runtime y validación ligera de `candidate_output` en [agent.py](<C:/Users/abaratas/Desktop/CARPETA PROYECTOS/TEST CODEX/NuevoProyecto/dist/copilots/cicd/langchain/agent.py:26>).

No había `.git`, así que creé snapshot previo en `.codex-loop/backups/cicd-runtime-contract-20260505-141148`. Los validadores también refrescaron reportes bajo `generated/`.

Verificación ejecutada y pasando:
- `python tools/validate_copilot_factory.py`
- `python tools/validate_prompt_quality.py`
- `python tools/validate_runtime_equivalence.py`

También comprobé JSON, compilación de LangChain y presupuesto de tamaño; retiré el `__pycache__` generado por `py_compile` antes de cerrar.
